title: Detecting Ladder Logic Bombs in IEC 61131-3 PLC Programs using ESBMC-PLC+ — Artifact
description:
Reproduction artifact for the paper Detecting Ladder Logic Bombs in IEC 61131-3 PLC Programs using ESBMC-PLC+: A Formal Verification Approach with Trigger Synthesis. The method uses ESBMC-PLC+ as its verification engine (unchanged) and adds a modeling layer (src/modeling_layer.patch) that exposes function-block-resident Ladder Logic Bomb (LLB) logic to the prover.

Includes: the modeling-layer patch, corpus generators (adaptive-adversary variants, a 310-program Boolean/integer corpus, a value-forgery example), a proof-of-concept, run harnesses, and an EXPECTED.md mapping each claim to a script. Third-party datasets are pinned at fixed versions for reproducibility: the Iacobelli PLC-LD-dataset (commit c2ee2324) and the PLC-Defuser SWaT development snapshot (commit 0361129) are bundled frozen, each with its upstream GPL-3 LICENSE and a SHA256 integrity manifest, while the archived PLC-Defuser SWaT v1.0.0 release (DOI 10.5281/zenodo.14014820) is fetched at run time. A single binary reproduces both the sound Boolean/integer configuration (zero false positives) and the analog-extended configuration via the --ld-sound-mode flag, with the scan-watchdog controlled by --ld-scan-watchdog/--ld-scan-budget.

Honest scope: strong on Boolean/integer ladder logic (30/30 third-party detection, 100% on the 310-program corpus, adaptive 5/5), with unbounded bomb-absence proofs and trigger synthesis. On PLC-Defuser's real analog SWaT benchmark the analog modeling extension parses the full corpus for the first time and detects 149/150 (99%) with zero false positives on the archived v1.0.0 release; on a later development version that adds nonlinear-arithmetic (i*i) non-termination bombs, detection drops to 49% as the SMT backend times out. Semantic model checking and CFG-triage are complementary.
